forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KY7E2TTAX937ZQ37N46ADPCH
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9580'
+title: '@Observable per-session state: phase, token counts, context meter'
+---
+Absorbs the harness's HarnessState and answers the FoundationModelsAgents plan §10 item 2c observable-transcript ask. Actors can't be @Observable, so each RoutedSession pairs with a main-actor @Observable projection published after isolated mutations: current phase (idle/generating/running-tool/compacting), running transcript, token counts, and the fill-fraction context meter (live mid-turn once the generate-boundary reporting lands). SwiftUI binds the projection; the actor stays the single writer.
\ No newline at end of file

 title: '@Observable per-session state: phase, token counts, context meter'
 ---
 Absorbs the harness's HarnessState and answers the FoundationModelsAgents plan §10 item 2c observable-transcript ask. Actors can't be @Observable, so each RoutedSession pairs with a main-actor @Observable projection published after isolated mutations: current phase (idle/generating/running-tool/compacting), running transcript, token counts, and the fill-fraction context meter (live mid-turn once the generate-boundary reporting lands). SwiftUI binds the projection; the actor stays the single writer.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,4 +1,29 @@
 ---
+comments:
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kyacekng60ww8ab0ahfgac3t
+  text: |-
+    Implemented via TDD.
+
+    Research: read SessionEvent.swift (SessionEvent/ToolCallStatus/TokenUsage), RoutedSession.swift (RoutedSession protocol, streamEvents doc comment on emission order), and SessionEventStreamTests.swift for the exact event shapes/ordering already landed by task 46adpch. Also read ResolutionProgress.swift (the only existing @MainActor @Observable type in this package) as the direct precedent — but its progress object is written into by Router.resolve via internal `await MainActor.run` hops, whereas SessionEvent.swift's own doc comment (added by task 46adpch) explicitly says a future "@Observable per-session projection (task ekd82f4)" *consumes* the SessionEvent vocabulary — i.e. it is a stream consumer, not something RoutedSessionActor pushes into internally. Went with the consumer design: zero changes needed to RoutedSession/RoutedSessionActor.
+
+    New file: Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Session/SessionProjection.swift — `@MainActor @Observable public final class SessionProjection`:
+    - `Phase` enum: idle/generating/runningTool/compacting, refreshed by whichever SessionEvent most recently arrived.
+    - `TranscriptEntry` (Identifiable via a synthesized ULID, for direct SwiftUI ForEach use) with a `Kind`: `.text`/`.reasoning` (coalesced across consecutive textDelta/reasoningDelta fragments into one growing entry), `.toolCall(ToolCallEntry)`, `.compaction(CompactionResult)`.
+    - `ToolCallEntry` (Identifiable by its own String id): name/argumentsJSON/status/summary, updated in place by a later toolStatus event correlated by id — two concurrent same-name calls stay distinct entries.
+    - `tokensIn`/`tokensOut`: cumulative across every turnEnded observed over the projection's whole lifetime (not just the latest turn) — a running token-usage counter.
+    - `contextFill`: latest measured value from the most recent turnEnded (live mid-turn across retries, per task g2hcm36's already-landed per-attempt stamping).
+    - `apply(_ event: SessionEvent)`: the core reducer, one event at a time.
+    - `apply(eventsFrom: AsyncThrowingStream<SessionEvent, Error>) async throws`: convenience draining a whole `streamEvents(to:maxTokens:)` call; resets to `.idle` in a `defer`, so a throw never leaves the projection stuck non-idle.
+
+    Updated SessionEvent.swift's own doc comment to link the prose "future @Observable per-session projection (task ekd82f4)" to the now-real `` ``SessionProjection`` `` type.
+
+    Tests (TDD, written first — confirmed a compile failure with `SessionProjection` unresolved before writing the implementation): new Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/SessionProjectionTests.swift, 13 tests — initial state; textDelta/reasoningDelta coalescing (including a kind-change starting a fresh entry rather than merging); toolCall+toolStatus(.running) entry creation; toolStatus(.completed) updating the matching entry in place; two concurrent same-name tool calls staying distinct by id; a toolStatus with no matching call being a safe no-op; compaction appending an entry and setting phase .compacting; turnEnded accumulating tokens across multiple calls (simulating an auto-compaction retry) and reporting the newest contextFill; apply(eventsFrom:) applying events in order and resetting to idle on both normal completion and mid-stream throw (rethrowing after applying everything yielded first). No router/profile/backend scaffolding needed since the type only consumes plain SessionEvent values / a hand-built AsyncThrowingStream.
+
+    Verification: baseline was 551 tests passing (verified via clean `swift test` run before starting, matching the prompt's stated baseline). Now 564 passing (551 + 13 new), 0 failures, 0 warnings. `swift build --build-tests` clean. `mcp__sah__diagnostics check working` — 0 errors/0 warnings.
+
+    Adversarial double-check agent dispatched next per really-done; will record its verdict before finalizing.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-24T15:38:37.616666+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KY7E2TTAX937ZQ37N46ADPCH
 position_column: doing

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -24,6 +24,21 @@

     Adversarial double-check agent dispatched next per really-done; will record its verdict before finalizing.
   timestamp: 2026-07-24T15:38:37.616666+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kyact3sbfn7r3ywb6xm4zaea
+  text: |-
+    Adversarial double-check (round 1): REVISE — one finding.
+
+    Design-intent check (item 1 in the review brief) came back confirmed: the "consumer" design (SessionProjection drains RoutedSession.streamEvents rather than RoutedSessionActor pushing into it internally) is well-grounded in SessionEvent.swift's own pre-existing doc comment, not a shortcut. No change needed there.
+
+    Finding (addressed): `updateToolCall`'s doc comment claimed a `toolStatus` event with no matching prior `toolCall` was "a no-op," but `apply(_:)` unconditionally set `phase = .runningTool` before calling it regardless of whether a match was found — so an untracked status event still flipped the observable phase (a bound SwiftUI view would show a "running tool" spinner with no corresponding transcript entry). The existing test only asserted `transcript.isEmpty`, never `phase`, so the gap wasn't caught.
+
+    Fixed via TDD: strengthened `toolStatusWithNoMatchingToolCallIsANoOp` to also assert `projection.phase == .idle`; confirmed it failed for the right reason (`projection.phase == .runningTool` instead) before fixing. `updateToolCall` now returns `Bool` (`@discardableResult`) reporting whether it found and updated a match; `apply(_:)`'s `.toolStatus` case only sets `phase = .runningTool` when `updateToolCall` returns `true`. Doc comment updated to state the guarantee precisely.
+
+    Verification (fresh): `swift build --build-tests` clean. `swift test` — 564 tests (541 + 18 + 5), 0 failures, up from the 551 pre-task baseline (+13 new `SessionProjectionTests`, no test count regression). `mcp__sah__diagnostics check working` — 0 errors/0 warnings.
+
+    Task left in `doing`, ready for `/review`.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-24T15:44:54.571820+00:00

 - 01KY7E2TTAX937ZQ37N46ADPCH

--- original

@@ -39,6 +39,19 @@

     Task left in `doing`, ready for `/review`.
   timestamp: 2026-07-24T15:44:54.571820+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kyad3bw7bvn0jhgde55ea7cj
+  text: |-
+    Adversarial double-check (round 2, bounded per really-done's contract): PASS.
+
+    Independently re-verified the round-1 fix is correct in every code path (only one path through `updateToolCall` returns `true`; `phase` is provably untouched on a miss), confirmed the strengthened test is non-tautological (traced the exact regression it would catch), and ran a fresh `swift build --build-tests` + `swift test` itself: 564/564 passing (541+18+5), 0 failures, 0 warnings.
+
+    One trivial doc-precision nit raised (non-blocking): `phase`'s and `Phase`'s doc comments said phase is "refreshed by every `apply(_:)` call" / "derived from whichever event most recently arrived," which is now an approximation given the untracked-toolStatus no-op case. Fixed since it was cheap and correct: reworded both doc comments in `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Session/SessionProjection.swift` to state precisely that an untracked `toolStatus` leaves `phase` unchanged, cross-referencing `updateToolCall`.
+
+    Final verification (fresh, this message): `swift build --build-tests` clean. `swift test` — 564 tests (541+18+5), 0 failures. `mcp__sah__diagnostics check working` — 0 errors/0 warnings.
+
+    Task is done and green. Leaving in `doing` for `/review`.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-24T15:49:57.767991+00:00
